title: Embed queries with the exact index model
description: Use the identical embedding model and version for queries that built the index, since mismatched encoders place vectors in incomparable spaces.
body: Use when issuing queries against a vector index. Do not embed the query with a different model, version, or pooling than the one that encoded the corpus — the two live in different spaces and similarity becomes meaningless. Actions: pin the embedding model id and version, route query and indexing through the same encoder, and verify a known query retrieves its known match. Validation signals include a query/index model-version match recorded in config and a smoke query returning its expected neighbor. Risk boundary: a silent encoder mismatch collapses recall with no error; if the index model version is unknown, deny querying and escalate to re-embed against a confirmed model.
